item.title: Version 0.1.27
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.27 — a clearer way into a project
When you act on a project, Odak now shows you exactly which one you're acting on.
### New - **Selected-project pill.** Drilling into a project's actions — with Tab or ⌘K — now pins it as a pill in the search bar, Spotlight-style, so the project you're acting on is always clear. Press Esc, click the pill's ✕, or hit Backspace to step back to your search exactly where you left it.
### Improved - **Tab still switches editors.** Tab keeps cycling through the open editor windows on a row (Enter jumps to the one you pick) — that didn't change. What's gone is the focus ring sitting on the first icon before you'd touched anything; rows read calmer until you actually Tab. - **Tidier popovers.** The actions and worktree popovers now keep their inner scroll corners in lockstep with the card's rounded edge.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.28
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.28 — your coding agents, on every surface
The biggest release since launch. Odak now sees your coding agents — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Droid, Amp, OpenCode — and puts their live status on every surface: search, the switcher, the notch. Start one with a keystroke, jump to the exact pane it's running in, and know the moment one finishes or needs your input. Around that: a full visual refresh, redesigned Settings and onboarding, and a long list of speed and reliability fixes.
### Agents - **Live agent sessions, tracked.** Connect your agents' hooks in one press (onboarding, or Settings → Agents) and Odak shows each session's state in real time — working, needs input, or finished — attached to the right project. Claude Code, Codex (CLI and the ChatGPT app), Cursor, Gemini, Droid, Amp, and OpenCode are supported. - **Start an agent with one keystroke.** Pick a project, choose an agent, and Odak opens it where it belongs: a split next to your existing zellij/tmux pane, a new tab in the host terminal, or your preferred terminal. Default launch flags per agent live in `~/.odak/agents.yaml`. - **Jump to the exact pane.** Focusing a session doesn't just raise the terminal — it lands on the zellij or tmux pane, terminal tab, or editor window actually hosting that agent. ⌥Tab now returns you to the session you last used. - **Cards instead of notification banners.** When an agent finishes or asks a question, a floating card appears — showing the real question and its options, not a generic "needs attention". Cards stack, stay out of the way, and a finished turn keeps a review badge until you've actually looked at it. - **The notch knows.** The island shows compact agent marks with status dots, and drops an alert when a session needs you — on the built-in display and external ones. - **Close sessions from the switcher.** ⌘W on a session row closes a Codex desktop thread or folds a terminal agent's pane, same as closing a window.
### New look - **The Volt design system.** A ground-up visual refresh: Manrope type, flat high-contrast panels, solid status badges, and a signature volt accent. Themes are replaced by one curated accent palette that drives selection, status, and glyph colors consistently in light and dark. - **A brushed-metal brand.** New app icon and in-app search mark, with automatic light/dark variants. - **Settings, consolidated.** Twelve focused tabs instead of a sprawl, native macOS chrome with pane titles and back/forward, and deep links from every surface. - **Onboarding that shows the product.** Setup steps are now mini desktop scenes built from the real product chrome — including an interactive ⌥Tab tryout and the one-press agent hook connect.
### Improved - **Near-zero idle CPU.** Hidden panels no longer render at frame rate in the background; a resting Odak now costs roughly nothing. - **Stale-app reaper (opt-in).** Odak can quit apps you haven't touched in N hours — with guards for the frontmost app, unsaved changes, and anything hosting a live agent session. Idle windows wear a zzz badge first. Off by default; enable in Settings → Quick Switcher. - **Search polish.** The query renders in solid ink with a cleaner completion ghost, the caret never displaces text, and the dashboard morph is choreographed instead of jumpy.
### Fixed - **Shortcuts work right after login.** Launching at login could leave every global shortcut dead until a manual relaunch. Odak now presents its panel host reliably at startup. - **Fullscreen focus rescue.** Jumping to a session or window hosted by a fullscreen iTerm or Xcode now lands correctly instead of bouncing off. - **Interrupted agents don't stick.** A Claude session you interrupt no longer badges "working" forever, and VS Code's empty Copilot stubs no longer appear as phantom sessions. - **Notch cursor.** Surfaces near the notch no longer inherit the cursor of the app behind them.
### Requirements - macOS 15 Sequoia or later · Apple Silicon.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.
